Japanese: ２月２３日　午後　２時１５分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: February 23, 2:15 PM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: あ、あの。成歩堂さん！ それで‥‥
English: Uh... um, Mr. Wright! So...

Japanese: ‥‥ケッキョク、 どうなっちゃってるんですか？
English: What's going on with the case, anyway!?

Japanese: あたし。なんだか コンランしちゃって。
English: I... I'm a little confused.

Japanese: え！　そ、それは‥‥その。 （ちょっと、整理してみよう）
English: Huh!? W-well, um... let's see. (What is going on?)

Japanese: ‥‥被害者は多田敷　道夫捜査官。 ２１日の午後５時すぎに、刺殺。
English: The victim, Detective Bruce Goodman, was stabbed to death after 5:00 PM on the 21st.

Japanese: 殺害現場は、検事局の地下駐車場。 ‥‥および、警察局の保管室、と。
English: He died in the Prosecutor's parking lot... and the Police Department's evidence room.

Japanese: なんですか“および”って！
English: What's this "and the evidence room" part!?

Japanese: 警察局と検事局‥‥ クルマで３０分は離れてますケド。
English: The Prosecutor's Office and the Police Department are 30 minutes apart by car.

Japanese: それを、これから調べるんだよ。 （調べてわかるモノなら）
English: Well... that's what we're going to find out. (Or try to, at least...)

Japanese: よおおおし、がんばるぞお！
English: Alright! Let's do it!

Japanese: （ヤル気なのはうれしいけど、 調査の役には立たないよな‥‥）
English: (Glad she's in good spirits, but I'm not sure she's going to be much help with this...)

Japanese: それはどうかな？　成歩堂さん。
English: Don't be so sure, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、これから つきあってもらえますか？
English: Would you mind coming with me?

Japanese: このメガネがダテじゃないコト、 見せるときが来ましたね！
English: I'll prove that these thick- rimmed glasses of mine aren't just for show!

Japanese: さあ、行きましょう！ いざ、カガクの道をッ！
English: Let's go! Science awaits us!

Japanese: 千尋さんが“チャーリー”と 名づけてかわいがっていた植物。
English: Mia's plant, "Charley."

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんがいなくなってからも、 こいつの世話だけは忘れない。
English: I've been taking care of him in Maya's absence.

Japanese: ‥‥あれ？ なんか今日は、妙に黄色いな。
English: Huh? He looks a little yellow today...

Japanese: あ。さっき、お水あげて おきました。たっぷり、混ぜて。
English: Oh, I watered him just now. I mixed in a lot, too.

Japanese: “まぜて”って‥‥ナニを？
English: Mixed in... what?

Japanese: あかねちゃん特製のカガク添加物。 どうですか？　チャーリーくん。
English: Why my very own scientific additive! Well, Charley? You like it?

Japanese: だから、妙に黄色いよ！
English: I told you, he's turning yellow!

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: あ。さっき、あたしも ベンキョウしてみたんですよ。
English: Oh, I tried studying one of those just now.

Japanese: ホラ。今日の法廷で モンダイになった‥‥
English: Remember what they were talking about in the trial today...?

Japanese: ああ、“証拠法”ね。 どう？　わかった？
English: Oh, right, evidence law. So, did you learn anything?

Japanese: ちょっと読んでみたら、 アタマが痛くなっちゃいました。
English: Well, when I tried reading it made my head hurt.

Japanese: はあ。
English: Oh...

Japanese: ついでに、しまうときに 足の上に落っことしちゃいました。
English: Then, when I closed it, it slipped out of my hand and fell on my foot.

Japanese: （好感の持てる子だな。 ‥‥意外と）
English: (Oddly enough I find myself identifying with her on this one...)

Japanese: 今日も向かいのホテルは 大はんじょうだ。
English: Looks like it's cleaning day again at the hotel across the way.

Japanese: なんでも今度、支店を 出すそうだ。
English: I hear they're planning a second branch outside the city.

Japanese: ‥‥ふと目をやったら、 あのボーイと目が合ってしまった。
English: Egad! The bellboy was staring right at me.

Japanese: なんか、コシが低いように見えて、 それでいて、キレがあるような。
English: He looks wimpy, but you can tell, there's a sharp edge under that facade.

Japanese: 将来、大物になりそうな フンイキのボーイさんですね。
English: That bellboy is going to be someone someday.

Japanese: （ぼくも言われてみたいもんだ）
English: (Wish someone would say that about me...)

Japanese: 千尋さんが使っていたデスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: 依頼を引き受けなくなってから、 ますます座る機会がへった。
English: I sit here even less now that I've stopped taking cases.

Japanese: あ。来週、英語のテストがあるから ベンキョウしないと！
English: Uh oh! I have a history test coming up next week! I'd better study!

Japanese: デスク、借りますね！
English: I'm borrowing this desk, okay?

Japanese: “土曜日は、来週まで ごぶサタデー”‥‥と。
English: Let's see... George Washington was the first king of America...

Japanese: （‥‥ごめん、千尋さん‥‥）
English: (Sorry, Mia...)

Japanese: トノサマンのポスターが 張ってある。
English: There's a poster of the Steel Samurai on the wall.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが、帰りぎわに 張りかえていったみたいだ。
English: Maya stuck it up here on the day that she left.

Japanese: せっかくの置きみやげだから、 そのままにしてあるんだけど‥‥
English: I didn't have the heart to take it down.

Japanese: たまに、お客さんの視線が 妙にイタいときがある。
English: I do sometimes get strange looks from the clients, though.

Japanese: ‥‥さてと。 何からとりかかろうかな。
English: Well... where should we begin?

Japanese: あ。それはやっぱり‥‥ 例のアレじゃないですか？
English: Oh, well, isn't it obvious? We should begin with that, y'know... that thing.

Japanese: ‥‥被害者のナゾ、だよね。 多田敷捜査官は、どうやって‥‥
English: The mystery of the victim I guess. How could one man, Detective Goodman...

Japanese: 警察局と、検事局で《同時に》 殺害されたのか？
English: be killed in two places simultaneously?

Japanese: あ。それはやっぱり‥‥
English: Oh, well, you see...

Japanese: 警察局の現場に行ってみないとね。 《証拠保管室》だったっけ。
English: We should go to the Police Department... the evidence room was it?

Japanese: ううう‥‥あたし、ここでは 必要とされていないのかな‥‥
English: Uh... I'm not being very useful here, am I...

Japanese: （冗談なのに‥‥）
English: (No, no! You're being very... helpful.)

Japanese: 御剣検事さん‥‥ カワイソウでしたね。
English: Poor Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: コマるんだよねー。御剣ちゃんさ。 ムズカシイ時期なんだから。
English: You are becoming a thorn in my side, Worthy... There've been rumors...

Japanese: 去年、被告席に立ったばかりだし。 他にもウワサ、聞いてるよ？
English: After all, you were in the defendant's chair just last year...!

Japanese: あの、オマワリさんの報告書の コトもありましたよね。
English: There was that business with him not reading that officer's report...

Japanese: ああ‥‥警察局の事件、だね。
English: Ah, about the killing at the Police Department, right.

Japanese: （巌徒局長‥‥ たしかに、その主張は正しい）
English: (The Chief of Police was right about that...)
(But I can't help but think...)

Japanese: （どこかに、御剣に対する “悪意”を感じるよな‥‥）
English: (Someone at the Police Department doesn't like Edgeworth...)

Japanese: ホラ。これが弁護士バッジだよ。
English: See this? It's my Attorney's Badge.

Japanese: ふむふむ。ほおほお。 これがウワサの‥‥
English: Ahh! Well! I've never seen a real one before.

Japanese: （こんなに食いついてくる子は 初めてだな‥‥）
English: (You're the first one who's actually been interested in mine, believe me.)

Japanese: 材質は銀。金メッキが少し はがれてきてますね。
English: Its composition is mostly silver. The gold plating is flaking a bit.

Japanese: （カガク的に分析されてしまった）
English: (She analyzed it. Scientifically...)

Japanese: 硫化物による腐食は、まだみたい。 ５０００円ぐらい、でしょうか。
English: There doesn't appear to be any corrosion due to sulfides. I'd give you $50.00 for it.

Japanese: 悪いけど、売る気はないよ。 ‥‥まだ。
English: Sorry, but it's not for sale. Yet.

Japanese: そのナンバーが キメ手だったんですよね？
English: That number was the clincher, wasn't it!

Japanese: 《５８４２１８９》‥‥ 記録に残っていたんだよな。
English: 5842189... It's in the record.

Japanese: あの日の５時１５分。 警察局で殺害された捜査官のＩＤ。
English: The ID number of the detective killed at the Police Department that day at 5:15.

Japanese: でも‥‥そのカードは、検事局の 地下駐車場で見つけたんですよね。
English: But... we found this card in the parking lot at the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: （ヒマなときに考えよう）
English: (Something to think about later when I'm not so busy.)

Japanese: あたしを置いて 逃げないでくださいっ！
English: Don't leave me here to do all the thinking alone!

Japanese: 凶器、ですよね。 御剣検事さんのナイフ‥‥
English: So... Mr. Edgeworth's knife was the murder weapon.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。おキョウさんが、 目撃しているんだよ。
English: Right... or so the witness, Ms. Starr, claims.

Japanese: ともえさんが‥‥このナイフで、 多田敷捜査官を刺したトコロを。
English: She saw your sister stab Mr. Goodman with this knife.

Japanese: うううう‥‥ゼンブ おキョウさんがワルいんですっ！
English: Ugh... You know, all of this is that Angel Starr's fault!

Japanese: ヒトのせいにしちゃダメだよ。
English: She's not the one on trial here.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥おキョウさんの 見マチガイですっ！
English: Well, no, but she must have seen it wrong!

Japanese: 気のせいにしちゃダメだよ。
English: Her eyesight isn't on trial here, either.

Japanese: 成歩堂さんもほしいですね。 《ベンゴ・オブ・ザ・イヤー》。
English: I bet you'd be pretty happy with a "King of Attorneys" shield, too, huh?

Japanese: 弁護士たちって、どうも まとまりがないからね。
English: We defense attorneys are a scattered, unrecognized bunch.

Japanese: それにしても‥‥どうして “割れた盾”なんでしょうねー。
English: Still... I wonder why the trophy shield is broken?

Japanese: たしかに、気になるなあ。
English: It is pretty odd, huh?

Japanese: （‥‥まさか、事件にカンケイ してるワケ、ないけどなあ）
English: (I doubt it has anything to do with this case though...)

Japanese: おキョウさんの証言によると ‥‥事件の、直後。
English: According to Ms. Starr's testimony...

Japanese: 彼女は、携帯電話を使っている。 相手は‥‥キミ、だね？
English: Ms. Skye used her phone right after the murder... she called you, right?

Japanese: あ、あの！ ‥‥すぐ、切れちゃったんです。
English: B-but...! She hung up right away.

Japanese: なんだか、デンパの調子も 悪くて‥‥よく聞こえなかったし。
English: And it was a bad connection. I couldn't hear anything.

Japanese: （地下駐車場だったから、かな）
English: (Because it was an underground parking lot, I suppose...)

Japanese: たしかに、“マフラー”のコト、 話してましたけど‥‥
English: She did say something about a "muffler"...

Japanese: すぐに、切れちゃったから‥‥
English: but got cut off...

Japanese: こ、こんな写真‥‥ おキョウさんの見マチガイですっ！
English: Th-this photograph... Ms. Starr must have been seeing things!

Japanese: イミがわからないぞ。
English: And photographing things?

Japanese: （事件現場に、たしかに ともえさんは、いた‥‥！）
English: (This proves Ms. Skye was there at the scene of the crime!)

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥事件にカンケイして いると考えるしかないよな）
English: (She is connected to this crime somehow... but how?)

Japanese: どうして、こんなものが クルマの排気パイプに‥‥
English: Why would this have been in the car's exhaust pipe?

Japanese: ムカシの事件の証拠品、 なんですよね？
English: It's evidence from an old case, right?

Japanese: そう、だね。《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥ 解決した事件みたいだけど。
English: Right... the SL-9 incident. It was already solved, apparently.

Japanese: （証拠品の《申し送り》の当日に 盗み出された証拠品‥‥）
English: (This knife was stolen on the day of the evidence transferal...)

Japanese: （少し、調べたほうがいいな。 《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥か）
English: (Maybe I should look into this "S-L9 Incident"...)

Japanese: 高いんですよー、コレ。
English: This stuff is pretty expensive.

Japanese: へえ。‥‥いくらぐらい？
English: You don't say. How much does it cost?

Japanese: そうですね。１グラム、 １０００円以上するんですよ！
English: I'd say about $10 per gram!

Japanese: おこづかいためて 買ったりするんですけどね。
English: I save up my allowance to buy it.

Japanese: “おこづかい”‥‥ あの。ともえさんからもらうの？
English: Your allowance...? Does your sister give you that?

Japanese: はい。 ‥‥両親はいないから。
English: Yes. We don't have parents...

Japanese: そう、か‥‥
English: R-right...

Japanese: たまに、現物支給のときも あるんですよ、おこづかい。
English: Sometimes she even gives me a bottle instead of my allowance!

Japanese: げ、げんぶつしきゅう‥‥？
English: A bottle of luminol testing fluid...?

Japanese: （おこづかいが、ルミノール試薬 ‥‥うれしくないなあ）
English: (Man, I'd just take the allowance myself...)

Japanese: あの。これ、なんだけど。
English: Here, see this?

Japanese: ふむふむ。やはり弁護士さんは 見せたがり屋である、と。
English: Ahh! I've noticed that defense attorneys have a tendency to want to show people things.

Japanese: （弁護士の生態をカガク的に カンサツされてしまった‥‥）
English: (What is this? A behavioral study of lawyers?)

Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 検事局・地下駐車場
English: February 23 Prosecutor's Office Underground Parking Lot

Japanese: あたし。警察局の事件は、 どうでもいいんです！
English: You know, I really don't think we should worry about the Police Department murder!

Japanese: だって。被害者の死体が 見つかったの、ココですからね！
English: There wasn't even a body found there! Who cares?

Japanese: （警察局で殺害されたのも 多田敷　道夫なんだけど‥‥）
English: (Of course it was our victim who was killed at the Department...)

Japanese: あたし‥‥信じられないんです。 あの、お姉ちゃんが‥‥
English: And my sister would never do such a thing! I know it...

Japanese: その“ドラム缶”‥‥ 中は、カラだったのだろうか？
English: That oil drum... Was it empty?

Japanese: 主席検事サマが倒したドラム缶‥‥ 水が入っていたよ。
English: The oil drum kicked over by the Chief Prosecutor... was brimming with water!

Japanese: ‥‥お姉ちゃんが、現場の痕跡を 消そうとするなんて！
English: My sister, erasing evidence at the crime scene? Never!

Japanese: （なんだかんだ言っても、 好きなんだな‥‥ともえさん）
English: (Even though she says they don't get along, Ema really likes her sister...)

Japanese: そ。そういうのじゃありませんっ！
English: That's not it at all!

Japanese: ただ‥‥あの。同じ“ぷろ”として その。信頼してるんです！
English: It's just...
We're both professionals at what we do... and I trust her!

Japanese: （高校生が何か言ってるぞ）
English: (Big words for a high school student.)

Japanese: ‥‥でも。血痕があったにせよ、 なかったにせよ‥‥
English: Well, whether there was blood stains or not...

Japanese: ドラム缶の水で、すべては消えた。 今さら、もう‥‥
English: The water in that oil drum washed it all away.

Japanese: ‥‥ふっふっふっ。
English: He he heh.

Japanese: あたしのカガクをナメたら カラダにドクですよ、成歩堂さん。
English: Ignore the strength of my science at your own peril, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥な。 なんだよ、その不敵な笑いは。
English: Huh? What's that grin for?

Japanese: そんなときこそ、コレの出番です。 ルミノール試薬ッ！
English: This situation calls for one thing, and that is... Luminol testing fluid!

Japanese: るみのーるしやく？
English: L-luminol?

Japanese: “血痕”って、ちょっと洗った ぐらいじゃ、消えないんですよ。
English: Blood is sticky stuff, you know. You can't just wash it away with a little water.

Japanese: ‥‥目には見えなくても、ね。
English: Even if you can't see it, it's still there...

Japanese: でも‥‥そんな検査なら やってるんじゃないかな。警察も。
English: But wouldn't the police have already done those tests?

Japanese: ダイジなのは、自分の目で たしかめるコトです！
English: Never trust anyone's eyes but your own, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ささ。‥‥とにかく、 やってみてくださいよ！
English: Just give it a try!

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくがやるのかよ！
English: M-me? Why do I have to do it!?

Japanese: だって、あたしホラ。 未成年だし。
English: I'm a minor! I can't even drink yet!

Japanese: （カンケイない）
English: (We're testing blood stains with this stuff, not drinking it...)

Japanese: じゃ、ホラ、ホラ。 このメガネ、かけるかける。
English: Here, look, I'll lend you these glasses.

Japanese: ‥‥え！　そのメガネ、 もう１本あったの？
English: Huh? You had an extra pair of those things?

Japanese: 血液反応を調べたいところに、 クスリを“ふきつける”んです！
English: To test for a blood reaction, just spray the luminol on it.

Japanese: ‥‥ホラ。こんなふうに。
English: Like this, see?

Japanese: 画面をタッチすれば、クスリを ふきつけますから。
English: Touch the screen to spray it on.

Japanese: さあ！　これで、 血痕を探してみましょうよ！
English: Okay! Let's find us some bloodstains!

Japanese: （‥‥メガネごしに、青白い 光が浮かび上がってきた‥‥！）
English: (I can see her eyes shining behind those glasses...)

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ これが“血痕”なの？
English: So, is this a bloodstain?

Japanese: ううううう‥‥ なんか、ナマナマしいよお。
English: Uhhhh... It's so... ugh!

Japanese: な、なんで 声がフルえてるんだよ。
English: Ema, you're shaking.

Japanese: じつは‥‥ジッサイに見るの、 あたしも初めてなんです。
English: It's just... this is my first time seeing real blood!

Japanese: （‥‥たよりない カガク捜査官クンだな）
English: (Scientific investigation in action...)

Japanese: と、とにかく。たしかに これは“血痕”ですけど‥‥
English: O-okay, well, we definitely know that this is a bloodstain.

Japanese: 何か、気になりませんか？ ‥‥ある種、カガク的に。
English: But, doesn't something strike you as odd? Scientifically speaking, of course?

Japanese: （“気になるコト” ‥‥カガク的に考えてみよう）
English: (What's odd about this... scientifically?)

Japanese: （“気になるコト” ‥‥カガク的に考えてみよう）
English: (What's odd about this... scientifically?)

Japanese: モチロン‥‥ 血痕の“位置”だね。
English: Why, the blood stain's location is odd! Elementary!

Japanese: え。ど、どうして “モチロン”なんですか？
English: H-huh? Why is that "elementary"?

Japanese: ハンニンと格闘があったのなら、 血痕があるのは自然ですよね？
English: I mean, I would think that if there was a fight, you'd expect some bloodstains here.

Japanese: ま。ある種、ね。
English: I suppose you might think that.

Japanese: もう！　シッカリしてくださいッ！
English: C'mon, Mr. Wright! We're all counting on you!

Japanese: （この“血痕”‥‥ たしかに、違和感がある）
English: (There is something odd about this bloodstain...)

Japanese: （“位置”でないとしたら、 その正体は‥‥）
English: (But if it's not the location of the blood, then maybe...)

Japanese: この違和感、もしかしたら‥‥ 血痕の“量”なのかな。
English: Maybe it's the amount of blood that's odd?

Japanese: ‥‥ハンニンと多田敷捜査官は、 ここで格闘したワケだよね。
English: The perpetrator and Detective Goodman fought here, right?

Japanese: そのワリには‥‥血痕の量が 少なすぎるような気がするな。
English: Don't you think there'd be a little more blood?

Japanese: あ！　あたしも、そう思います！
English: I definitely think so!

Japanese: だって‥‥被害者のクツの底には、 こんなに血がついてるのに！
English: I mean...
Look at all the blood on the sole of the victim's shoe!

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥不自然だ！）
English: (It's strange!)

Japanese: （ここで格闘があったのなら、 血の足跡がなければオカシイ！）
English: (If they fought here, there'd have to be more bloodstains than this.)

Japanese: あの。あの、あたし！
English: Uh, h-hey, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 血痕を見つけたら、こうして 上面図に書きこんでいきますね！
English: See how I'm marking up the floor plans when we find a blood stain?

Japanese: ‥‥‥ね。 なかなかヤルでしょ？　あたし。
English: See... I'm pretty handy to have around, right?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ベンリだね。 ものすごく。
English: Uh, yeah. That's very... useful information to have.

Japanese: おこづかいためて 買ったんですからね、コレ。
English: I saved up my allowance to buy this!

Japanese: 《ルミノール試薬》のスプレーを あかねちゃんにジマンされた。
English: Luminol Testing Fluid information received from Ema.

Japanese: 警察は、すべてのデータを 提示してくるとはかぎりません。
English: We can't be sure that the police will reveal all their evidence in court.

Japanese: ツゴウの悪い証拠は、 ダマってることがあるんです。
English: Sometimes they fail to mention evidence that doesn't fit with their view of the case.

Japanese: その、“かくされた証拠”を 自分たちで暴き出す‥‥
English: And we'll drag that "hidden evidence" out into the light of day!

Japanese: ね。ね。“やってやる！”って 気になるでしょ！
English: Yeah!
It feels like we're really investigating a crime now, doesn't it?

Japanese: （気になるところがあったら 使ってみよう‥‥コイツ）
English: (This luminol stuff is going to come in handy.)
Hah!

Japanese: そのクスリ‥‥サシミ弁当にも 反応するのかしらね。
English: I wonder how that fluid of yours would react to a nice Deli Box?

Japanese: お‥‥おキョウさん‥‥！
English: Ms. Starr!

Japanese: “信じられるのは、自分の目” 気に入ったよ、アンタ。
English: You only trust your own eyes, hm? Not bad, you two...

Japanese: 血ぬられた、このサシミ弁当‥‥ おキョウのオゴリですわ。
English: This day-old Deli Box is on the house...

Japanese: ‥‥すみませんけど、 その紹介じゃ食べられません。
English: Sorry, it's just, that kind of lead in doesn't really get my mouth watering.

Japanese: （“事件”に関わる捜査官たちが 消え、“証拠品”が現れた‥‥）
English: (Just when the detectives on the case have disappeared, we find new evidence...)

Japanese: 無関係‥‥じゃありませんよね。 ゼッタイ！
English: There has to be a connection!

Japanese: ‥‥ボウヤ。
English: So, Rookie...

Japanese: な。なんですか。
English: Wh-what!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥この事件、ホンキで 調べるつもりのようだねぇ。
English: It seems like you're serious about investigating this case...

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。
English: Yes.

Japanese: これを持っておゆきなさいな。
English: Then you should take this.

Japanese: ステーキ弁当‥‥ですか。
English: A... Salisbury Steak Lunch?

Japanese: エサにつられて、協力してくれる オトコがいるだろうさ‥‥
English: I know a certain guy who might help you if you tempt him with this treat...

Japanese: おキョウさんから 《ステーキ弁当》を受け取った。
English: Steak Lunch received from Ms. Starr.

Japanese: あの‥‥！
English: Um, Ms. Starr...?

Japanese: おキョウさんと、罪門さんは、 その‥‥
English: Officer Marshall... is he your... uh, are you his...?

Japanese: つつつつつつつきあって らっしゃるんですかッ！
English: Are you g-g-g-going out!?

Japanese: ‥‥どうして、そんなコトを？
English: Why do you want to know?

Japanese: 罪門さん‥‥ どうしちゃったのかな、って。
English: I was just wondering what happened to him?

Japanese: ムカシ‥‥お姉ちゃんと組んで 捜査していたころは、やさしくて。
English: A long time ago, when he was helping my sister do cases, he was so nice.

Japanese: あのふたり、とても仲がよくて ‥‥うらやましかったんです。
English: He got along so well with my sister, it made me jealous.

Japanese: それに‥‥あたしのコトも、 かわいがってくれたのに。
English: And... he was nice to me too, back then.

Japanese: （罪門さんが、捜査官だった ころのハナシ、かな‥‥）
English: (This would be when Officer Marshall was a detective.)

Japanese: それなのに、今になって。 なんか、冷たいんです‥‥
English: But now... now he's so cold!

Japanese: キョウスケとアタシは、 協力して調査をしているだけ。
English: Jake and I are merely cooperating on this investigation.

Japanese: ‥‥過去に決着をつけるために。 それ以上のコトは、わからないね。
English: We're putting the past to rest, as it were. Nothing more than that.

Japanese: そう、ですか。 ‥‥ありがとうございます。
English: I... I see. Thank you.

Japanese: （罪門　恭介‥‥か）
English: (Officer Jake Marshall... Hmm...)

Japanese: ここは‥‥クルマの出口ですね。
English: This is where the cars leave the lot.

Japanese: 床のヤジルシを見ると、 “入口”みたいだけどね。
English: The arrow on the ground makes it look more like an entrance.

Japanese: ナニ言ってるんですか！ どう見ても出口じゃないですか！
English: What are you talking about? It's plainly an exit!

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥出口であると同時に、 入口でもある、というコトかな。
English: Well, maybe it's both. Kind of a dual purpose?

Japanese: あ！　それ！　あの有名な “ソータイセー理論”ですね！
English: Ah hah! The theory of relativity!

Japanese: え！　は、はあ‥‥
English: What? Uh...

Japanese: メモしとかないとね‥‥ あ！　ね。ね。成歩堂さん。
English: I've got to write this down. Ah! Hey, hey, Mr. Wright! Maybe you know...

Japanese: どこの国のヒトでしたっけ？ ソー・タイセーさんって。
English: Was Mr. Relativity, German? Or was he British?

Japanese: ‥‥知らないよ。
English: Mr. Relativity? Are you sure that was his name?

Japanese: あ。ハシゴですよ。
English: Ah hah! A ladder.

Japanese: それはキャタツだよ。
English: Um... That's a stepladder.

Japanese: どこが違うんですか？ カガク的に証明してください。
English: What's the difference? In scientific terms, please.

Japanese: か、カガク的‥‥
English: S-scientific huh?

Japanese: もっとホンシツを見ましょうよ、 成歩堂さん。
English: Look at the basic nature of the thing, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （なぜだろう‥‥あまずっぱい キモチになってきた）
English: (This all seems so horribly familiar somehow...)

Japanese: お。こんなところに イミありげなドアが！
English: Look! A door! This must mean something!

Japanese: ドアに“イミ”なんてないよ。
English: I'm not sure that doors "mean" anything.

Japanese: クッ！　開きません！ ナゾのカギがかかってます！
English: No! It won't open! A mysterious lock!

Japanese: カギに“ナゾ”なんてないよ。
English: I fail to see what's mysterious about it.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さん。 もっと人生を楽しみましょうよ。
English: Mr. Wright... You need to learn to enjoy life more.

Japanese: 先に調査をすませようよ。
English: Let's finish our investigation first, shall we?

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥ あそこから見たんですね‥‥事件。
English: So that's where Ms. Starr saw the incident from.

Japanese: 高いぶん、フツウよりも よく見えたかもしれないね。
English: You can probably see quite a lot from up there.

Japanese: （そうなると‥‥）
（“彼女が刺したのを見た”と いう証言も、ウソとは思えない）
English: (That probably means she wasn't lying when she saw Ms. Skye stab him...)

Japanese: そもそも‥‥警備員は ナニをしてたんだろう。
English: Where's the security guard, anyway?

Japanese: ええと。あたしが 聞いてきたトコロによると‥‥
English: Well, this is just something I heard...

Japanese: どうやら、おキョウさんのお茶を 買いに行ってたみたいです！
English: But apparently he went out to buy coffee for Ms. Starr.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (That woman is a force to be reckoned with...)

Japanese: これが‥‥モンダイの ドラム缶だね。
English: So, this is the famous oil drum.

Japanese: ちょっと、ケリ倒してみますね。 ここはひとつ、あたしも。
English: Well, no time like the present! I'll try to kick it over myself!

Japanese: えいっ！
English: Hii-yah!

Japanese: な、泣かないでほしいな。 （ともえさん‥‥スゴいパワーだ）
English: Th-that's okay. Don't cry. (That Lana Skye must be a powerful woman...)

Japanese: これが‥‥あの “ついたて”ですね。
English: So, this is the famous divider.

Japanese: コイツのおかげで崩すコトが できたからなあ‥‥今日の証言。
English: It sure helped us knock a hole in that testimony today.

Japanese: あるイミ、このカベこそが ホンモノの証人だったのかもね。
English: Come to think of it, this divider helped our case more than the actual witness.

Japanese: 《カベに耳あり、正直メアリー》 ‥‥まさに、そんな感じですね。
English: The great divider! A chip off the old parking block!

Japanese: メアリーはないよ、あかねちゃん。 （今の子は知らないのかな、障子）
English: It's just a wall. (Scientifically speaking...)

Japanese: お。電話がありますよ。 ‥‥どれどれ。
English: Here, a phone. Let's see if it works...

Japanese: こらこら。 勝手にさわらないほうがいいよ。
English: Hey. Don't touch stuff we don't need to be touching.

Japanese: な、何も聞こえません！
English: I can't hear anything!

Japanese: あたしの耳、ヘンになったのカモ！ 気圧のせい、でしょうか‥‥
English: My ears! No, my ears! Maybe it's due to the barometric pressure...

Japanese: （ナニを、バカなコトを‥‥）
English: (What is she babbling about?)

Japanese: あ、そこ！　なんですか！
English: Hey!? What did you just say?

Japanese: 聞こえてるじゃないか！ 電話がコワれてるんだよ！
English: See? You can hear just fine. The phone's broken!

Japanese: まっかなすぽーつかーですね。
English: That's about as red as a sports car can get.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。ジツにまっかだ。
English: Yup, it's pretty red alright.

Japanese: （アイツのクルマから 被害者の死体が発見された）
English: (The body was found in Edgeworth's car trunk...)

Japanese: （トランクのカギも コワされている）
English: (And the lock on the trunk was broken too...)

Japanese: （ともえさんは‥‥なぜ、 アレを選んだのか‥‥）
English: (So, the question is why did Ms. Skye chose his car?)

Japanese: で？　で？　なんていう クルマなんですか？　アレ。
English: So, what model car is it?

Japanese: “セダン”か“クーペ”だと 思うんだ。クルマの名前なら。
English: I think it was called a "Sedan" or a "Coupe." Something like that.

Japanese: ‥‥それ、車種です。 成歩堂さん、オトコの子でしょ！
English: Those are car types, Mr. Wright. Not models.
You're a guy, aren't you, Mr. Wright? Your supposed to know these things!

Japanese: （この事件が解決したら、 免許をとろう‥‥）
English: (Maybe it is about time I got my driver's license...)

Japanese: 《Ａブロック》‥‥ 検察関係者のエリアですね。
English: "A Block"... This area is reserved for prosecutors.

Japanese: ぼくは弁護士だから、 《Ｂブロック》になるのかな。
English: Defense attorneys are relegated to "B Block."

Japanese: いつかあたし、ここに クルマをとめるのがユメなんです！
English: I dream of the day when I will be able to park my car here!

Japanese: それで、Ｂブロックの成歩堂さん から、ハンバーグ弁当を買うの。
English: I'll go over to B Block to buy my hamburgers from you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥とうぶん、弁護士を やめるつもりはないよ。
English: I'm not planning on giving up my job that soon...

Japanese: この向こうが《Ｂブロック》 ‥‥外来者用のエリアだね。
English: "B Block" is through there. That's where visitors park.

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥ この金網を乗り越えたんですか！
English: So... Ms. Starr climbed over this fence!?

Japanese: そ、そうみたいだね。 （３メートル以上あるぞ‥‥）
English: I-it seems so, yeah. (That fence is 9 feet high at least!)

Japanese: ちょっと、のぼってみますね。 ここはひとつ、あたしも。
English: Well no time like the present! I think I'll give it a little try!

Japanese: きゃあっ！
English: Eeeek!

Japanese: だ。だから、泣かないでほしいな。 （おキョウさん‥‥さすがだな）
English: I-it's okay, don't cry. (Maybe there's a Lunchland Olympics team?)

Japanese: このロープ‥‥やっぱり？
English: This rope... is it...?

Japanese: うん。被害者の死体のカタチを ロープでなぞってあるんだよ。
English: Yep. They laid it in the outline of the victim's body.
So wait...

Japanese: 被害者‥‥トランクを閉めるとき、 挟まって死んだのでしょうか！
English: The victim must have died when the killer closed the trunk on him!

Japanese: （こんな発想をする子、 他にはゼッタイいないだろうな）
English: (You have got to be the only person I know that would come to that conclusion.)

Japanese: ここにあったんですよね。 ‥‥このメモ。
English: That's where we found that note.
The SL-9 Incident.

Japanese: 事件があった日、《申し送り》に なるはずだった事件。
English: A case that was due for transferal the day of the murder.

Japanese: （その事件を担当していた 捜査官が、殺害された‥‥）
English: (And the detective in charge of the case was the victim.)

Japanese: どんな事件だったんでしょうね。 《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥
English: I wonder what kind of case this SL-9 Incident thing was?

Japanese: 事件の分類ナンバーだけ聞いても、 想像もつかないよ。
English: It's kind of hard to guess just knowing the case number.

Japanese: やってくれましたわね。 ‥‥今日は。
English: You certainly put me in a tight spot today.

Japanese: すみません。でも‥‥
English: My apologies Ms. Starr, but...

Japanese: わかっておりますわ。 ワルいのは、こっち。
English: No, no, it's okay. It was my fault.

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥
English: Oh, we know.

Japanese: ワタクシが事件を目撃したのは、 あの守衛室のガラスごしでしたわ。
English: I witnessed everything from that security room right there.

Japanese: でも。それを言ったら、証言が ウス味になってしまう‥‥
English: But... I was afraid that wouldn't sound convincing enough, you see...

Japanese: どうしても、こいクチしょうゆを 足したかった‥‥ごめんあそばせ。
English: I was wrong to think that. I'm sorry.

Japanese: ひ。ヒドいじゃないですか！ ウソをつく、なんて‥‥
English: Sorry? You lied on the witness stand! That's unforgivable!

Japanese: いいかい、おじょうちゃん。 忘れてないだろうねェ‥‥
English: Little girl, don't forget what's important here.

Japanese: 目撃した場所はちがっても、 おキョウはハッキリ“見た”のさ。
English: Even if the place I witnessed the events from was different, I still saw what I saw.

Japanese: 宝月主席検事の、殺人の瞬間を！ ‥‥この証言はまだ、生きている！
English: I saw Chief Prosecutor Skye stab a man in cold blood, and that testimony still stands!
Ah...

Japanese: 捜査官として、誓ってもいい。 あの女は、多田敷を刺したのさ！
English: I swear it on my honor as a detective! She stabbed Goodman!

Japanese: （この写真‥‥その“イミ”を カクニンしたほうがいいな‥‥）
English: (I know this photograph has something important to tell us... but what?)

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥ 刑事、だったんですね。
English: So... you were a detective, weren't you, Ms. Starr?

Japanese: ２年前まで。ナツカシイ話ね。
English: Yes... It was a long time ago. Well, two years ago.

Japanese: コワモテの容疑者の方々も、 一度、ワタクシの前に出たら‥‥
English: No matter how hardened the criminal, when they faced me...

Japanese: コドモみたいにゲロしたもんさね。
English: They coughed it up.
Coughed it... up?

Japanese: “自白”のコト。
English: They confessed.

Japanese: みんな、洗いざらいしゃべり ちらかしたモノですわ。
English: They babbled like babies.

Japanese: なにしろ‥‥こう見えてワタクシ、 天女にもなれますのよ。
English: You know, I may seem like a demon sometimes... But I can be an angel, too.

Japanese: ‥‥そうみたいですね。
English: I wouldn't doubt it.

Japanese: 彼らの、ばっちいハナシを ずるずる引きずり出すうちに‥‥
English: Every day, I dragged the dirt out of the mouths of suspect after suspect...

Japanese: いつしか、こう呼ばれていましたわ ‥‥《ゲロまみれのおキョウ》。
English: And before long, they called me... The Cough-up Queen!

Japanese: おキョウさんは、真実のかぐわしい カオリを身にまとっている、と。
English: Oh, and here I thought someone had gotten food poisoning from your lunches.

Japanese: たしか、解雇‥‥ “クビ”になった、とか。
English: And... you were "let go"? Er... fired?

Japanese: 私、捜査官を天職とココロ得て おりましたわ。おそらく‥‥
English: I felt that I had found my dream job when I became an investigator...

Japanese: そこのすました検事さんたちに 解雇通知を受けなければ‥‥今も。
English: And if these prim and proper prosecutors hadn't let me go, I'd still be one today.

Japanese: あの事件のせい、さ。 ‥‥《ＳＬ９号事件》。
English: It's all because of that case... The SL-9 Incident.

Japanese: え、えすえる‥‥！ （もっと詳しく聞けないか‥‥？）
English: Ess... el..? (Wait! She doesn't mean...!)

Japanese: ‥‥思い知らされましたわね。
English: That's when I learned the truth.

Japanese: ワタクシたちは、しょせん 《捨てゴマ》にすぎない‥‥
English: We're nothing to them. Disposable.

Japanese: すてごま‥‥
English: Disposable?

Japanese: ２年前のコトでしたわ。 あれは‥‥大きな事件だった。
English: Two years ago... it was the biggest case I'd ever handled.

Japanese: ワタクシたちは、決定的な証拠を つかもうと、必死でしたわ。
English: The police and the prosecutors were desperate for decisive evidence.

Japanese: 解決‥‥しなかったんですか？
English: So... they didn't solve it?

Japanese: ‥‥いいえ。
English: On the contrary.

Japanese: キレイさっぱり解決しましたわ。 犯人は逮捕され‥‥処刑された。
English: It was solved quite cleanly. The criminal was caught and executed.

Japanese: （しょ、処刑‥‥）
English: (E-executed...?)

Japanese: 結果には、モンダイはない。 犯人は当然のムクイを受けたのさ。
English: Yes, the criminal got what was coming to him. It doesn't get any cleaner than that.

Japanese: ‥‥ただ、モンダイは‥‥ 決定的な証拠は、なかった。
English: The only problem was... they never did find decisive evidence. Not even a little.

Japanese: え！　‥‥でも、ハンニンが 処刑されたというコトは‥‥
English: What!? But the criminal was executed, right?

Japanese: ‥‥“作られた”証拠品。
English: Evidence... of a sort. Made up evidence.

Japanese: そ、それって‥‥それって！ まさか、“ねつ造”‥‥ですか？
English: Wh-what? You mean they executed someone with fabricated evidence!?

Japanese: 事件がカタづいて、数ヶ月後。
English: The best part came several months after the trial.

Japanese: 捜査に関わっていた刑事は全員、 なんらかの理由で“整理”された。
English: Every detective involved with the case was dealt with.

Japanese: ある者は、巡査へ格下げされ、 ある者は‥‥免職になったり。
English: Some were demoted to patrolmen, others found themselves out of a job...

Japanese: それが‥‥おキョウさん、ですか。
English: And... you were one of those?

Japanese: ‥‥アナタたちがよく ご存じの殿方もいらしてよ。
English: Myself, and one other person you know well.

Japanese: （もしかして‥‥）
English: (Wait, could it be...?)

Japanese: そう。罪門　恭介捜査官。
English: Exactly. Officer Jake Marshall.

Japanese: ‥‥おっと。今は、 署内保安課の巡査さんだっけねェ。
English: He's on security detail in the Police Department, isn't he?

Japanese: アタシたちは刑事として、 あの事件を必死に捜査した‥‥
English: As professional detectives, we investigated that case from every angle.

Japanese: ‥‥特に、キョウスケの執念は スゴかったねェ。
English: Jake was particularly determined.

Japanese: そして、事件が終わって‥‥ アイツは巡査に格下げされた。
English: And then... it was over. And he was demoted.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: However...

Japanese: アイツは、今も忘れていない。 そして‥‥このアタシも。
English: He hasn't forgotten. And neither have I!

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥ですか。
English: You haven't forgotten SL-9?

Japanese: あの事件には“ウラ”があった。 アタシたちは、それを追っている。
English: There was another side to that case, a hidden side. That's what we're after now.

Japanese: 上層部の連中に、それを 止めるコトはできないのさ。
English: And no one up in their fancy offices can stop us.
Wait!

Japanese: あ！　も、もしかして そのお弁当は‥‥
English: Th-those lunches you sell...

Japanese: アタシが、この呪われた検事局に 弁当を売りに来る理由は、１つ。
English: There is only one reason I come to sell lunches in this accursed office.

Japanese: 捜査にチカラを貸してくれる、 ムカシの男たちに会うためさ。
English: I come here to meet old friends... boyfriends that can help me investigate.

Japanese: （おキョウさんの“カレ”か‥‥）
English: (Ms. Starr's old boyfriends... How many does she have, anyway?)

Japanese: タクアンの化石、かしら。
English: That looks like a petrified piece of provolone.

Japanese: い、いえいえ！ 弁護士バッジですよ！
English: Huh? I-it's not! It's my attorney's badge!

Japanese: タクアンの化石のほうが 価値がありますわね。
English: I'd think a petrified piece of provolone would fetch more on the open market.

Japanese: アイニクですけど、 売る気はないですから。‥‥まだ。
English: Look, my badge isn't up for sale. Not yet, at least.

Japanese: ２年前までは‥‥ ワタクシも持っておりましたわ。
English: I had one of those up until two years ago...

Japanese: あ。そうか。ゲロまみれだった ワケですからね！　当時は。
English: Back when you were the Cough-up Queen, right?

Japanese: このＩＤ、この駐車場で 見つけたんですけど‥‥
English: We found this ID card here in this parking lot...

Japanese: マチガイございませんわね。 多田敷のモノですわ。
English: Well, there's no mistaking that. It's definitely Goodman's.

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: 警察局で殺害された捜査官の ＩＤとも、一致してるんですよ。
English: It's the same ID as the man who was killed at the Police Department.

Japanese: あり得ませんわね。
English: That's impossible.

Japanese: （こんなふうにバッサリ 割り切れれば、ラクだよなあ）
English: (I wish I could be so sure...)

Japanese: 御剣のクルマから、御剣のナイフが 刺さった死体が発見された‥‥
English: A body with Edgeworth's knife stuck in it was found in Edgeworth's car.

Japanese: ワタクシにカンシャして いただきたいものですわね。
English: I'd think he owes me his gratitude.

Japanese: カンシャ‥‥？
English: Gratitude?

Japanese: ワタクシが事件を目撃して いなかったら‥‥
English: Why, if I hadn't witnessed the crime...

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事さんが タイホされていたかもっ！
English: Mr. Edgeworth would have been the suspect!

Japanese: （それは‥‥ないと思うけど）
English: (Hmm... I wonder.)

Japanese: しかし、なぜ‥‥主席検事サマは 凶器を用意しなかったのかねェ‥‥
English: Still, it's strange.
Why didn't our Chief Prosecutor have her own murder weapon ready?
Um... What do you think about this?

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥ このナイフに‥‥そして、メモ。
English: "SL-9 Incident"... On that knife...
And on that note...

Japanese: 多田敷はねェ‥‥
English: Goodman...

Japanese: あの事件の捜査担当のセキニンシャ ‥‥刑事部長だったよ。
English: Goodman was the head detective on that case, you know.

Japanese: そうだったんですか‥‥
English: Really?

Japanese: このナイフは、あの事件の証拠品 ‥‥“凶器”だった。
English: That knife was evidence from that case... the murder weapon.

Japanese: まさに、多田敷が殺された日に 《申し送り》される予定だった‥‥
English: It was due for transferal the very day that Goodman was killed.

Japanese: やっぱり。あの事件は まだ、終わっちゃいないのさ！
English: As I suspected...
SL-9 isn't over! Not yet!

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥もっと、 詳しい情報をいただけませんか？
English: Do you think you could tell us more about the SL-9 Incident?

Japanese: ワタクシが主席検事サマの ウデをつかんだとき‥‥
English: It was when I grabbed the Chief Prosecutor on the shoulder...

Japanese: 彼女はそれをアスファルトの上に 落としましたのよ。
English: She dropped that phone on the pavement.

Japanese: そのとき聞いたんですか。 “マフラー”のヒトコトを。
English: That's when you heard her talking about the "muffler," right?

Japanese: そうですわね。まさか‥‥ あんなワナがあったとは、ねェ。
English: Little did I know... it was a trap!

Japanese: 赤い検事サマのクルマのマフラーと 主席検事サマの赤いマフラー‥‥
English: The red car's muffler, and the prosecutor's red muffler!

Japanese: （ともえさん‥‥本当は、 何を伝えたかったんだろう？）
English: (What was Ms. Skye really trying to say, I wonder?)

Japanese: 考えてみれば‥‥写真は、 守衛室からでも撮ることができた。
English: If you think about it, I could have taken that picture from the guard room.

Japanese: でも。さすがのワタクシも、 ほんの少し、浮き足立ちましたの。
English: But... even I get flustered sometimes.

Japanese: だから‥‥とっさに、 現場に向かったわけですね？
English: So, you went straight to the scene of the crime?

Japanese: 犯行を止めるために、 金網まで乗り越えて‥‥
English: And climbed the chain link fence in an effort to stop the murder?

Japanese: そのとき撮ったのが、この写真。
English: That's when I took this photo, yes.

Japanese: つまり‥‥事件発生から ５分ほどたった、“あと”‥‥
English: In other words... five minutes after the crime?

Japanese: その“５分”がモンダイだった。 証言の“スキ”になるからねェ。
English: Those five minutes are the whole problem...
The hole in my testimony, as it were.

Japanese: で、でも！　ホントのコトを 証言するべきでした！
English: The five minutes weren't the problem, Ms. Starr, you lying was the problem!

Japanese: アタシはねェ、おじょうちゃん。 ‥‥もう、二度と！
English: Listen, little girl. I've had my testimony "disregarded" before...

Japanese: 自分の証言が“なかったコト”に されたくなかっただけ、なのさ。
English: And I wasn't going to have it disregarded again!

Japanese: あの事件のときのように‥‥
English: Just like that time...
(That time...?)

Japanese: キョウスケにわたしなさいな。 協力してくれますわよ‥‥きっと。
English: You'll give that to Jake like I asked, won't you?

Japanese: あの‥‥！
おキョウさんと、罪門さんは、 その‥‥
English: Y-you, and Officer M-Marshall, you... uh...

Japanese: つつつつつきあって らっしゃるんですかッ！
English: A-a-a-a-are y-you... Are you going out!?

Japanese: キョウスケとアタシは、 協力して調査をしてるだけですわ。
English: Jake and I are cooperating on an investigation, that's all.

Japanese: ‥‥過去に決着をつけるために。 それだけのコトさ。
English: We're just putting the past to rest.

Japanese: そう、ですか。 ‥‥ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you.

Japanese: これ、見ていただけますか？
English: Could you take a look at this?

Japanese: ‥‥そちら。
English: You!

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Y-yes!?

Japanese: アガリは、ほうじ茶で よろしかったかしら。
English: You said you wanted some hot tea, right?

Japanese: あ、どうも‥‥ （見る気はないみたいだな‥‥）
English: Uh, no, but thanks. (She didn't even look at it.)

Japanese: うん。とってもオイシイ！ 深炒りでしか出ない味ですよねー。
English: Mmm! You must have to brew the leaves a long time to get rich flavor like this!

Japanese: 炒ったときのケムリで、 茶葉をいぶしてますのよ。
English: We "pre-infuse" the leaves with steam before brewing.

Japanese: やっぱり！　香ばしさのヒミツは そこにあったんですねー！
English: I knew it! So that's the secret to their aroma! Exquisite!

Japanese: （‥‥なんなんだ、 この香ばしい空気は‥‥）
English: (The only thing I'm smelling here is wasted time...)

Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 警察局・エントランス
English: February 23 Police Department Entrance

Japanese: ‥‥きのうより、さらに あわただしい気がするね。
English: It's even busier here today than it was yesterday.

Japanese: 刑事さんたち、目にも止まらぬ スピードで走りまわってますよ。
English: The detectives are running around so fast they're blurring.

Japanese: （警察局内で、捜査官が殺害 されたんだ。‥‥ムリもないな）
English: (I suppose it makes sense-- a detective did get killed in their own department.)

Japanese: こっちの現場‥‥“証拠保管室” でしたよね、たしか。
English: So... the evidence room. The scene of the crime!

Japanese: 受付でもらったパンフレットに よると‥‥あ。こっちですよ！
English: According to the pamphlet we got at the front desk...
Here it is!

Japanese: （遊園地に来たコドモみたいだな）
English: (She's like a kid at an amusement park.)

Japanese: ちょっと、のぞいてみましょうよ！ サツジン現場。
English: Ooh, a real crime scene! Let's go take a look!

Japanese: 今日もうごめいてますね、 タイホくん。
English: The Blue Badger is still writhing around today.

Japanese: “おどっている”と 言ってあげようよ、せめて。
English: Let's give him the benefit of the doubt and say he's dancing.

Japanese: （“おどっている”といえば‥‥）
English: (Speaking of "dancing"...)

Japanese: （警察局全体が、おどり狂ってるん だよな‥‥きのうから）
English: (The whole Police Department has been dancing around like crazy since yesterday...)

Japanese: ‥‥デンチ、抜いて おきませんか、アレ。
English: Can I take out his batteries?

Japanese: いつか、ナニか悪さを やらかしそうな気がします！
English: I just can't help but feel he's going to do something naughty!

Japanese: 刑事さんたちが見える。 みんな、忙しそうだ。
English: The detectives in there look pretty busy.

Japanese: あのドアの向こうで、 今、まさに！
English: Just imagine! Right now...

Japanese: くり広げられているんですね。 ‥‥“刑事ドラマ”がッ！
English: Behind those doors...! A police drama in action!
(Somehow the thought fails to excite me...)

Japanese: （たしかに、マチガイではないけど ‥‥ミョーにモヤモヤするな）
掲示板には、おなじみの ポスターが張ってある。
English: The usual wanted posters are hanging up on the bulletin board here.

Japanese: 《このカオに、ピン！　と来たら １１０番》‥‥か。
English: «Do you know this face!? If you do, dial 911!»

Japanese: ‥‥でも。 こういうポスターのカオって‥‥
English: You know, Mr. Wright, I've always thought it was kind of funny...

Japanese: なーんか、 ピン！　と来ないんですよねー。
English: I've never seen anyone who looked like the people in these posters.
They hardly even look human!

Japanese: （たしかに、マチガイではないけど ‥‥ミョーにモヤモヤするな）
English: (She has a point...)

Japanese: 《真冬の防犯フェア・開催中！》 ‥‥って、書いてあります。
English: The banner here is announcing the "Crime Fighting Campaign."

Japanese: 大ざっぱなスローガンだな。
English: Nice slogan...

Japanese: ルミノール試薬とか、 安く売ってないかなあ。
English: I wonder if they'll be selling fingerprinting sets.

Japanese: ‥‥バーゲンセールじゃ ないからね。
English: I don't think it's that kind of campaign.

Japanese: 家庭に１本あると、 安心だと思うけどなあ。
English: What family wouldn't want a set at home?

Japanese: ‥‥サツジン事件が 起きたとき、とか。
English: It's good for finding out who snuck into the cookie jar.

Japanese: 安心のためなら、他のものを 買ったほうがいいと思うよ。
English: I think most families can figure that out without the extra help.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　パトカーの色、 なんでシロクロか知ってますか？
English: Mr. Wright! Do you know why patrol cars are painted black and white?

Japanese: 知らないよ。どうして？
English: No idea. Why?

Japanese: あたしね。アレ、おむすびを デザインしたんだと思うんですよ。
English: Well, I think they're designed after a panda!

Japanese: お、おむすび‥‥？
English: A panda...?

Japanese: まあ。カガク的なコンキョは ありませんけどね。
English: Not that I have scientific proof. It's just a theory.

Japanese: ‥‥ちなみに、 どうしてそう思うの？
English: Um... do you mind me asking how you came up with that theory?

Japanese: 小学生のころ、遠足で見学に来た とき、パッとヒラめいたんです！
English: It was when I was on a school trip! I saw a patrol car and it came to me!

Japanese: ‥‥お弁当の時間に。
English: We had just been at the zoo, see...

Japanese: 今度、パンダを見に行くときは、 サンドイッチにしたほうがいいよ。
English: What about zebras? Or did they not have those at your zoo?

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　警察のマーク、 どういうイミか、知ってますか？
English: I always get excited when I come to the police station.

Japanese: 知らないよ。なんなの？
English: Why is that?

Japanese: あたしね。コレ、宇宙ステーション だと思ってたんですよ。ムカシ。
English: It just feels like I've jumped into a movie.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 小さいコロの話ですけど。 ホラ、カタチが似てるでしょ？
English: You know, with all the police and criminals.

Japanese: あ‥‥ああ。見たことないから。 ‥‥宇宙ステーションは。
English: W-well, I don't know if this is all that exciting.

Japanese: でも、“それはあり得ない”って 気づいたんです。去年。
English: Sure it is! Look at those two officers over there.

Japanese: だって、“ポリスステーション” ですからね。“警察署”は。
English: They're probably talking about the latest bust!

Japanese: もっと早く気づきたかったね。 それも、別のカタチで。
English: Funny, I thought they were talking about the weather.

Japanese: おまわりさんがケーレイしている。 相手は刑事さんだろうか。
English: Look, that patrolman is saluting that other guy. He must be a detective!

Japanese: バカ野郎！　オマエ、 どこに目ェつけてやがるんだ！
English: You idiot! What were you thinking!? Where's your head?

Japanese: はァッ！ ここと、ここでありますッ！
English: S-sir, it's r-right here sir.

Japanese: ケーレイじゃなくて、目の位置を カクニンしてるみたいですね。
English: I guess he wasn't saluting, he was showing the detective where his head was.

Japanese: ‥‥いいコンビになりそうだ。
English: They make a good pair.

Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 警察局・エントランス
English: February 23 Police Department Entrance

Japanese: （あいかわらず‥‥ピリピリした 空気が、ただよっているな‥‥）
English: (This place is charged with frantic energy, as always.)

Japanese: ‥‥くださーい！
English: Please!!!

Japanese: あれ。この声‥‥
English: Huh? Wasn't that...

Japanese: ステーキ弁当ひとつ、くださ‥‥‥ アレ。アンタたち。
English: One steak lunch, please! Oh, it's you.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事！
English: Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: ハナシはあとッス！ 刑事のハナをナメちゃダメッス！
English: Now's no time for chit-chat, pal. I'm a busy man!

Japanese: とりあえず、《ベントーランド》の ステーキ弁当を‥‥
English: What I really need is a steak lunch from Lunchland.

Japanese: あの‥‥コレ、ですか？ 売りモノじゃないんですけど。
English: Oh... you mean one of these? Actually, it's not for sale...

Japanese: （“ガックリ”という音が 聞こえてきそうだぞ）
English: (I think I just heard the sound of his heart breaking.)

Japanese: いや！　落ちこむのはアトッス！
English: Now's no time for despair!

Japanese: ハンニンがタイホされた、今！ 必要なのは、ショーコッス！
English: We've caught our criminal! Now we just need evidence!

Japanese: は、ハンニン‥‥ どういうコトですか！
English: The criminal... you mean...?

Japanese: 警察局の証拠保管室で起きた 事件のことは、聞いてるッスね？
English: You heard about the stabbing in the Police Department evidence room, pal?

Japanese: 検事局の駐車場で捜査官が 刺殺された、同じ日に‥‥
English: On the same day that a detective was killed in the Prosecutor's parking lot...

Japanese: 警察局でも、捜査官が 殺害されたというのですか！
English: Another detective... was killed at the Police Department!?

Japanese: それで‥‥ハンニンは？ 目星はついたのだろうか。
English: And the perpetrator? Do you have a suspect?

Japanese: まあね。容疑者はさ。 タイホしたのよ。さっき。
English: Well, there was a suspect. Just arrested 'em, in fact.

Japanese: もー、警察局をひっくり返す 大スキャンダルッス！
English: It's the biggest scandal to hit the station in ages! Everything's topsy-turvy.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事！　それで‥‥ いったい、ハンニンは‥‥！
English: But, Detective Gumshoe, who was it?

Japanese: とにかく！　ステーキ弁当が 食えないと知った、今！
English: Listen, pal, all I know is I need me a steak lunch, pronto!

Japanese: ここでオシャベリしてても、 ハラがへるだけッス！
English: Standing around here talking isn't going to fill my belly!

Japanese: あ！　ちょ、ちょっと。 待ってください！
English: W-wait! Don't leave!

Japanese: 詳しく知りたければ、 留置所に行ってみるッス！
English: If you want to know more, head on down to the detention center, pal.

Japanese: 取り調べが一段落して、 独房ですすり泣いてるハズッス。
English: Questioning should be over, so I figure he's down there having a good cry.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃッ！
English: Later!

Japanese: ‥‥証拠保管室のほうへ 走っていっちゃいました‥‥
English: He ran off to the evidence room...

Japanese: どうやら、ものすごいイキオイで 事件が動き出したみたいだね。
English: Well, this investigation is off to a running start.

Japanese: おまわりさんがケーレイしている。 相手は刑事さんだろうか。
English: Look, that patrolman is saluting that other guy. He must be a detective!

Japanese: 答えてみろ！ コレ、何本に見える？
English: Answer me! How many fingers do you see!?

Japanese: わからないでありますッ！
English: I-I'm sorry! I don't know!

Japanese: 右、０．４で、左０．２‥‥ メガネをかけたまえッ！
English: Well, that's 20 for the left, but nowhere near that for the right...
Get glasses, officer!

Japanese: ケーレイじゃなくて、 視力検査をしてるみたいですね。
English: That wasn't a salute... it was an eye test!

Japanese: ‥‥いいコンビになりそうだ。
English: They make a good pair.
